_description: Schema reference for app/pipeline_descriptions.json — the single, hand-authored source of pipeline description text. Shared across every UI variant (flowchart, cockpit) and every workspace: unlike pipeline_map.json (per-variant) and pipeline_cards.json (per-workspace-per-variant), this file has exactly ONE copy in the repo, sitting directly under app/ (not inside any app/<variant>/ subfolder) since it belongs to no single variant.
_generation_instructions: 1. This file is hand-authored by a human (Giulia), not AI-generated — agents should not invent or rewrite description text here without being asked to.
2. Keyed by pipeline `id` (the Python function name), the same join key used in pipeline_map.json and pipeline_cards.json.
3. A short paragraph is expected (a few sentences), not a one-liner — this supersedes the old per-node `description` field that used to live in pipeline_map.json.
5. Adding a new pipeline to the map? Add its id here too, or the app falls back to an empty description for that node.
_formatting:
Values support a small Markdown-lite subset, rendered client-side by app.js (function mdLite): **bold** or __bold__, *italic* or _italic_, and single newlines become
line breaks. Nothing else — no headers, links, lists, or raw HTML; anything that isn't one of those markers is escaped and shown literally.

In native (non-HTML) contexts — e.g. a card's title="..." tooltip attribute — the markers are stripped instead of rendered (function stripMd), so tooltips show plain text while the sidebar/panel shows the formatted version.
In JSON, write a literal newline inside the string as \n, e.g. "First sentence.\nSecond sentence.".
_i18n: Bilingual (English / French). Each description value is a { "en": ..., "fr": ... } object; both hold the same Markdown-lite prose, one per language.

The cockpit variant resolves the active language via pickLang() and falls back to `en` (then to ""). The flowchart variant is English-only for now and reads `en` via its descText() helper — so both variants keep working after the upgrade to the nested shape.
Backward compatible: a plain string value is still accepted and treated as `en`. New/edited entries should use the { en, fr } object form.
When adding a pipeline, provide BOTH `en` and `fr`. French drafts are authored/reviewed by Giulia (agents translate on request, per CLAUDE.md — do not invent description prose unprompted).
_deploy_note: Each UI variant is deployed as its own OpenHEXA static webapp (separate origin), so app.js can only same-origin fetch('./pipeline_descriptions.json'). This repo file (app/pipeline_descriptions.json) is the single source, but it must be copied into BOTH app/flowchart/ and app/cockpit/'s deploy bundle (files_json) at deploy time — same content, deployed twice, alongside the variant's other 4 generic files. Never hand-edit the deployed copies independently; always edit app/pipeline_descriptions.json and redeploy both variants.

_schema.version: string — date (YYYY-MM-DD), bumped when descriptions change

descriptions.<pipeline_id>: { en, fr } object — hand-authored, Markdown-lite-formatted paragraph per language, shown in the sidebar for this node (see _formatting and _i18n). A plain string is also accepted for backward compatibility and treated as `en`.
